page: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing premium remote storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D4als_v6 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for both stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core performance is moderate, registering average results in stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core suites. However, memory performance presents a clear bottleneck; the Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 10%, and read-only memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given its hardware profile and performance characteristics, the D4als_v6 is suited for workloads that require high single-threaded CPU performance but have low memory footprints. Examples include static web serving, text processing, and lightweight Redis operations, which all achieved average to strong benchmark ratings. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference due to its limited 8.0 GB memory capacity and weak memory bandwidth. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low memory allocation relative to the dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources positions this instance as a budget-conscious option for compute-centric tasks that do not demand large memory arrays.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it supports premium remote storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests. However, memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores ranking in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, text processing, and lightweight Redis operations, but is not recommended for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded tasks.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance featuring strong single-core performance optimized for low-memory workloads and budget-conscious deployments.

Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM and no local storage
